pattern: Because every operation always starts from the top-left corner, the cells incremented by all operations form the rectangle bounded by the minimum of every operation's two dimensions.
hints: Each operation increments a top-left rectangle, so the most-incremented cells are those covered by every operation.
The overlap of all operations is the rectangle whose dimensions are the smallest row bound and smallest column bound seen.
Take the minimum of all first coordinates and the minimum of all second coordinates; their product is the answer, or the full grid if there are no operations.

edge_inputs.description: no operations means whole grid is maximum
operation equal to full grid keeps full overlap

stress.description: random grid with random valid operations within bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randrange(1, 121)
    n = rng.randrange(1, 121)
    cnt = rng.randrange(0, max(1, size) + 1)
    ops = [[rng.randrange(1, m + 1), rng.randrange(1, n + 1)] for _ in range(cnt)]
    return [m, n, ops]
stress.note: small grid keeps the brute-force oracle simulation fast; each op respects 1<=a<=m and 1<=b<=n
